@graph.name: Jaguar F-Pace 30t / P300 Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar F-Pace 30t / P300 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F-Pace 30t/P300 engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. Customers have reported dealer quotes of around £15,000–£30,000 for engine replacement, while reconditioning or rebuilding is often in the £8,000–£10,000 range. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar F-Pace 30t / P300 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the model year. 2019 is the critical model year — Jaguar addressed timing chain and fuel pump flaws in all Ingenium engines from 2019 onwards. Pre-2019 models have well-documented timing chain guide failures. Post-2019 engines are considered more reliable. Check the Era Map above for your specific engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar F-Pace 30t / P300?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Timing chain guide failure on pre-2019 models — causing cold-start rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (2) PCV valve failure — causing excessive oil consumption, and (3) Turbocharger bearing wear — causing whining noises and power loss.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar F-Pace 30t / P300 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £15,000–£25,000 F-Pace, a £4,500–£5,500 recon is 20–35% of the car's value. For a clean post-2019 example, replacement is almost always worth it. For pre-2019 models with no chain history, evaluate the car's overall condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar F-Pace 30t / P300 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 30t/P300 uses the PT204 (also known as AJ200P) — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine producing 300 PS and 400 Nm. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the PT204 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain failure (£5,500–£8,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The full fix was completed from 2019 onwards, so post-2019 Ingenium engines should be fine.

rows.eraNote: 2.0L four-cylinder Ingenium petrol — 300 PS, 400 Nm. Timing chain and VCT issues on pre-2019 models

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar F-Pace 30t / P300 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an F-Pace 30t/P300 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The transverse engine layout adds complexity.

cta.label: → Compare F-Pace 30t / P300 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Guide Failure & VCT Issues
problems.symptoms: Rattling or knocking noise from the engine, especially on cold start, engine warning light, rough running, loss of power, misfires

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine's timing chain is a known weakness. The 2-litre weak point is the timing chain guides which do fail leading to catastrophic engine failure between 40 and 80 thousand miles. Jaguar addressed timing chain and fuel pump flaws in all Ingenium engines from 2019 onwards. A Variable Camshaft Timing (VCT) unit internal seal below specification can also cause rattling and engine warning lights. At the first sign of cold-start rattling, get the chain inspected immediately. A £1,500–£3,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£8,500 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: PCV Valve Failure (Excessive Oil Consumption)
problems.symptoms: Excessive oil consumption, blue smoke from exhaust, rough idle, engine warning light, oil spots under the car
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if oil consumption increases or smoke appears

problems.recommendation: A faulty PCV valve can cause excessive pressure in the crankcase, forcing oil into the combustion chamber and causing it to be burned. This leads to increased oil consumption and potential engine damage if not addressed. If you notice blue smoke from the exhaust or are regularly topping up oil, have the PCV system inspected. Catching this early prevents more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full PCV valve failure guide → /failures/pcv-valve-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Turbocharger Whine & Bearing Wear
problems.symptoms: High-pitched whining or siren-like noise from the engine bay, especially under acceleration, loss of power, smoke from exhaust

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you hear whining or experience power loss

problems.recommendation: F-Pace 30t turbos often whine due to bearing wear and boost pressure leaks. Turbo whining typically indicates worn turbocharger bearings or boost leaks. If you hear a high-pitched whine or siren-like noise, inspect the turbocharger's shaft play and listen for unusual sounds. A £1,200–£2,500 repair is far cheaper than the engine damage that can follow if ignored. Regular oil changes with the correct specification are essential for turbo longevity.

failureLink.label: Read the full turbo failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: F-Pace 30t / P300 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Piston-valve contact means full replacement required
Catch early to prevent engine damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This F-Pace 30t / P300? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2019 model (timing chain and fuel pump flaws addressed)
* Full service history with regular oil changes using correct 0W-20 spec
* No cold-start rattle — this is the critical warning sign
* Documented timing chain replacement on pre-2019 models
avoidIf: * Pre-2019 model without timing chain history — this is a ticking time bomb
* Cold-start rattle — indicates timing chain or VCT issues
* Excessive oil consumption or blue smoke — PCV valve failure
* Poor service history — the Ingenium petrol is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The F-Pace 30t/P300's PT204 Ingenium petrol engine offers impressive performance — 300 PS and 400 Nm in a stylish luxury SUV. However, early engines (pre-2019) have well-documented timing chain guide failures, with the 2-litre weak point being the timing chain guides which do fail leading to catastrophic engine failure between 40 and 80 thousand miles. Post-2019 engines are considered more reliable, with Jaguar addressing timing chain and fuel pump flaws. Buy a post-2019 model with full service history, or budget for timing chain work on earlier examples. Without history? Proceed with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare F-Pace 30t / P300 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar F-Pace 30t or P300 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or knocking from the engine (timing chain warning — this is critical)
* Timing Chain History — Has the chain been replaced? Pre-2019 models need this documented
* Service History — Including the PT204 engine and confirmed oil change intervals (0W-20 spec)
* Oil Consumption — Ask about oil usage; excessive consumption suggests PCV valve issues
* Turbo Health — Listen for whining or siren-like noises under acceleration
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for VCT or timing-related codes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
* Recall Compliance — Check for outstanding recalls on the vehicle
